Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. An interactive map of FLock Cams 138 upvotes, 17 comments
  2. Moss is a pixel canvas where every brush is a tiny program 69 upvotes, 7 comments
  3. NanoGPT Slowrun: Language Modeling with Limited Data, Infinite Compute 48 upvotes, 7 comments
  4. Faster C software with Dynamic Feature Detection 18 upvotes, 1 comments
  5. Qwen3.5 Fine-Tuning Guide – Unsloth Documentation 173 upvotes, 48 comments
  6. Who Writes the Bugs? A Deeper Look at 125,000 Kernel Vulnerabilities 39 upvotes, 7 comments
  7. MyFirst Kids Watch Hacked. Access to Camera and Microphone 76 upvotes, 20 comments
  8. Libre Solar – Open Hardware for Renewable Energy 143 upvotes, 44 comments
  9. Motorola GrapheneOS devices will be bootloader unlockable/relockable 1154 upvotes, 468 comments
  10. RE#: how we built the fastest regex engine in F# 171 upvotes, 58 comments
  11. “It turns out” (2010) 179 upvotes, 64 comments
  12. The Space Race's Forgotten Theme Park 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  13. Government grant-funded research should not be published in for-profit journals 274 upvotes, 124 comments
  14. The 1,700-year-old megastructure history almost forgot 14 upvotes, 2 comments
  15. Something is afoot in the land of Qwen 311 upvotes, 149 comments
  16. RFC 9849. TLS Encrypted Client Hello 241 upvotes, 118 comments
  17. A CPU that runs entirely on GPU 222 upvotes, 109 comments
  18. Agentic Engineering Patterns 430 upvotes, 238 comments
  19. Nobody Gets Promoted for Simplicity 681 upvotes, 400 comments
  20. Making Firefox's right-click not suck with about:config 121 upvotes, 69 comments
  21. Glaze by Raycast 149 upvotes, 90 comments
  22. Raspberry Pi Pico as AM Radio Transmitter 34 upvotes, 15 comments
  23. Data Has Weight but Only on SSDs 18 upvotes, 7 comments
  24. Emails to Outlook.com rejected due to a fault or overzealous blocking rules 99 upvotes, 66 comments
  25. Greg Knauss Is Losing Himself 59 upvotes, 37 comments
  26. TikTok will not introduce end-to-end encryption, saying it makes users less safe 361 upvotes, 352 comments
  27. My Favorite 39C3 Talks 7 upvotes, 2 comments
  28. MacBook Neo 1097 upvotes, 1417 comments
  29. Father claims Google's AI product fuelled son's delusional spiral 3 upvotes, 1 comments
  30. Roboflow (YC S20) Is Hiring a Security Engineer for AI Infra 0 upvotes, 0 comments
